title: The Olive Branch

jokes.text: And, there she is! Hot off the presses! Alexis Rose. Why is there a line through it? Oh, toner must be low.
jokes.setup: Proud presentation of official college certificate
jokes.punchline: The certificate has a literal line through the name due to poor printer maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of a proud moment with a mundane technical failure. Well-structured with clear setup and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, this is your official college certificate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that a line through my name?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the printer hasn't been serviced in quite some time.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So it literally says I didn't go to college.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Technically, it says you did go to college, there's just a line through it.

jokes.text: This certificate reminds me of the one you were awarded for getting your braces off. Now that was a grand day.
jokes.setup: Comparing college degree to another 'achievement'
jokes.punchline: Treating orthodontic completion as an award-worthy accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Johnny's earnest but misguided attempts to be supportive and celebratory.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A college degree is a major achievement, but so is completing orthodontic treatment.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, those are not the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Why not? You wear the brackets for years, you follow all the rules, you finally get them off, and you have something to show for it.

jokes.text: Yes, except my orthodontist had a slightly better printer.
jokes.setup: Comparison between college certificate and orthodontist certificate
jokes.punchline: The orthodontist having superior printing quality undermines the college's professionalism
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback that builds on the previous joke while adding Alexis's signature dry wit.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, my orthodontist certificate has way better printing than my college diploma.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's because your orthodontist takes their credentials seriously.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did David spend four months earning a degree?
jokes.setup: Sibling rivalry over recognition
jokes.punchline: Highlighting the achievement while undermining David's lack of accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic comedy that feels authentic to these characters' competitive relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I just got offered my own PR firm, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's... that's great, Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know it is. And you're still just... doing whatever it is you do at the store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I own the store, Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Exactly.

jokes.text: Pubic relations. - What? - No. - Oh my god, give me that!
jokes.setup: Reading an official college degree aloud
jokes.punchline: Typo turns 'Public Relations' into 'Pubic Relations'
jokes.explanation: Classic typo humor that gets big laughs but isn't particularly sophisticated. The reactions sell it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It says here you have a degree in Pubic Relations.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? No, it's Public Relations. There's a typo on the degree.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's certainly a credential that precedes you.

jokes.text: Is it though, do you think?
jokes.setup: Moira insisting it's obviously a misprint
jokes.punchline: David genuinely questioning whether it might be intentional
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his deadpan skepticism about whether this could be real is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's obviously a misprint.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Is it though? What if it's intentional?

jokes.text: So you're wearing a leather sweater, in the dead of summer.
jokes.setup: David's outfit choice
jokes.punchline: The impractical fashion choice highlighted matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good character observation that highlights David's fashion-over-function mentality.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, you can't wear that.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Because you have pockets. And pockets are a cry for help.

jokes.text: Okay, I just wanted to make sure that I was ready, ready.
jokes.setup: Explaining delay in confronting Patrick
jokes.punchline: The redundant 'ready, ready' reveals his over-anxious state
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing David's neurotic overthinking through verbal repetition.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was going to confront him sooner, but I wasn't ready. I wasn't ready. Okay, I wasn't ready.

jokes.text: So you waiting a full week to forgive him has nothing to do with the fact that he showered you with gifts and attention every day. That is purely coincidental!
jokes.setup: Alexis calling out David's motivations
jokes.punchline: David's obviously insincere denial of enjoying the gift treatment
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with Alexis seeing through David's self-deception about his motives.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You just like being treated like royalty.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I do not enjoy being treated like royalty.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, you literally just said—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I enjoy being treated like *me*. There's a difference.

jokes.text: Yeah, I think it's that, uh, and I think it's the fact that you've been a smidge needy this past week? - Hmm. - Maybe needy's the wrong word. - Hmm. - Clingy?
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why people want David to work things out
jokes.punchline: The progression from 'needy' to 'clingy' while David's non-responses show his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation structure with David's increasingly uncomfortable 'hmms' as the commentary gets more pointed.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: People think you guys should work things out because you're obviously still in love with each other.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not... I don't think that's...

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Like, first you were just texting him a lot, which was cute, then you were showing up at his place, which was a little needy, and now you're just... following him around.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not following him.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You literally followed him to the farmer's market today.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I needed produce.

jokes.text: And I am very excited to not, not be with him anymore.
jokes.setup: David expressing his feelings about reconciliation
jokes.punchline: The double negative creating confusion about what he actually wants
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's flustered state through linguistic confusion.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not saying I don't not want to reconcile with you.

jokes.text: That said, if a package does arrive, please keep it safe.
jokes.setup: David denying he's motivated by gifts
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking Alexis to protect any incoming packages
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy reveal - David immediately contradicting his denial with practical gift-protection concerns.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not motivated by gifts.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, can you please make sure to protect any incoming packages?

jokes.text: And what, pray tell, might this collection of words be?
jokes.setup: Alexis presenting her business proposal
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious dismissal of the document as merely 'words'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira pomposity - treating a business proposal like it's beneath her intellectual consideration.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Words? David, she's printed words on paper. How pedestrian.

jokes.text: Wow! Now, don't get me wrong, there is no High-Street beret that I would rather have thrown in my ring, but, I have a funny feeling Council already has its favourite.
jokes.setup: Responding to Alexis throwing her hat in the ring
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors with 'High-Street beret' and 'thrown in my ring' while dismissing the competition
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira word salad - mixing fashion terms with idioms in a way that's both pretentious and nonsensical.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, if you're throwing your High-Street beret in the ring, then I suppose I'll have to dust off my competition gloves. Though I must say, the competition around here is rather... pedestrian.

jokes.text: Darling, I realize the pickings are slim in this rural spanandry, but you can't ask Council to misuse public funds just so that you can find yourself a new boy toy.
jokes.setup: Critiquing Alexis's singles night proposal
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'spanandry' and reducing the business proposal to personal dating needs
jokes.explanation: Another Moira vocabulary invention ('spanandry') combined with her dismissive attitude toward Alexis's romantic life.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, darling, this isn't a business proposal, it's a cry for help wrapped in spanandry.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's not even a word.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It is now. And it describes exactly what you're doing here.

jokes.text: As the Irish like to say, it's a cinch to mash the Murphy where there's love.
jokes.setup: Moira offering encouragement with supposed Irish wisdom
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical fake Irish saying that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity - confidently delivering complete nonsense as if it's profound cultural wisdom.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, there's an old Irish saying: 'May your socks never get damp and your eyebrows stay exactly where you put them.'

jokes.text: Well, this is the second month. I won it last month, took myself out for lunch.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Employee of the Month program
jokes.punchline: Revealing he won his own award and celebrated alone
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sad and funny reveal about the desperate state of the motel's employee recognition program.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have instituted an Employee of the Month program at the store. It's a wonderful way to boost morale and recognize excellence. And I'm proud to say that I won it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I celebrated alone in my office with a store-brand sparkling cider and a single cupcake I bought from the grocery section.

jokes.text: I'm kidding. It's a little incentive for a job well done.
jokes.setup: The absurd self-award story
jokes.punchline: Claiming it was a joke while the truth is probably even sadder
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's attempt to save face while revealing his loneliness.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I was just joking about the award. I didn't actually give it to myself.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, that's somehow worse.

jokes.text: It's like a briefcase full of makeup.
jokes.setup: Stevie examining Johnny's gift
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact description of an absurdly excessive gift
jokes.explanation: Stevie's dry delivery makes the excessive gift seem even more absurd through understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You got me a car?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's not just any car. It's a Range Rover.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Johnny, this is insane. I can't accept this.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Nonsense. You deserve it. Besides, I got one too.

jokes.text: Aren't you forgetting something? - Uh, I just... just don't know how to thank you. - Oh, that smile on your face is all I need.
jokes.setup: Johnny expecting gratitude for his gift
jokes.punchline: Stevie's reluctant, forced thanks and Johnny's oblivious satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Johnny completely missing Stevie's discomfort and feeling proud of his terrible gift.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well? Aren't you going to thank me?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Thank you, Johnny.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're welcome. I knew you'd love it.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

jokes.text: In fact, by saying nothing at all, you... you spoke volumes.
jokes.setup: David claiming Patrick didn't say something specific
jokes.punchline: The ironic notion that silence was more communicative than words
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing how people read meaning into lack of communication.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You didn't say that.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I didn't have to. Sometimes what you don't say says more than what you do.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's insane. Words are literally designed to communicate.

jokes.text: After a glud of unasinous ideas put forth today, the room is suddenly bombilating with anticipation.
jokes.setup: Moira commenting on the council meeting
jokes.punchline: Multiple made-up or misused words: 'glud,' 'unasinous,' 'bombilating'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira vocabulary showcase with multiple invented words delivered with complete confidence - signature character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The council meeting was absolutely glud. I've never seen such unasinous support for the new initiative, and frankly, the bombilating energy in that room was simply transcendent.

jokes.text: good luck on getting your mitts on a Von Schlegell! Am I right, guys?
jokes.setup: Moira's elaborate art park proposal with famous sculptures
jokes.punchline: Roland's folksy dismissal using 'mitts' and acting like everyone knows this is impossible
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Moira's pretensions and Roland's down-to-earth reality check.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I hate to break it to ya, but you can't just put a bunch of fancy sculptures in the middle of town. This isn't some big city. We got our mitts full just keeping the roads from turning into rivers every time it rains.

jokes.text: Now, if an idea as simple as that can work for a people as infamously disorganized as the Scotch, surely we can aspire to something of equal scale.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to dismiss the singles idea while promoting her own
jokes.punchline: Calling Scottish people 'the Scotch' and claiming they're 'infamously disorganized'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira cultural ignorance and casual prejudice delivered with complete authority.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I think we should abandon this whole singles idea. It's pedestrian. Instead, we should focus on a sophisticated demographic. You know, like the Scotch. They're infamously disorganized, which means they'll be grateful for any structure we provide.

jokes.text: Single people is what this town needs! Loose wallets, loose inhibitions, everything's just really loose.
jokes.setup: Advocating for the singles event
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetition of 'loose' creating unintended sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with unintended sexual implications that Ronnie seems oblivious to.

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: You need to get these people out there! They're loose! They need to be loose! Let them be loose!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I understand the intention, but perhaps we could use a different word?

dialogue.speaker: Ronnie
dialogue.line: Loose! Loose! These people need to get LOOSE!

jokes.text: And a few pages of temporary letterhead. And um, a list of Buzzfeed's most motivational quotes for girl bosses under 30.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her business receipts
jokes.punchline: The progression from legitimate business expense to cliché internet inspiration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's millennial approach to business mixed with genuine effort.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, so these are my business receipts. This one is for a domain name, very legitimate. This one is for a photoshoot with a professional photographer. And then... it just devolves into screenshots of inspirational quotes I found on Pinterest and paid someone to design into graphics.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you're writing off motivational posters as a business expense?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: They're for the brand, David. My brand.

jokes.text: Did someone mention the posture thing? - What? - What is the utility shirts?
jokes.setup: Alexis asking if something's off about her appearance
jokes.punchline: Stevie immediately jumping to unrelated appearance issues
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Stevie revealing there are multiple appearance issues Alexis doesn't know about.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Is there something off about my appearance?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, your hair extensions are showing, your spray tan is uneven, and those false lashes are doing that thing where they're separating from your eyelid.

jokes.text: Like he built David a basketball court for his Bar Mitzvah. - Oh boy, okay. - Mm hmm. And unfortunately, the only way to like, train it out of him, is to show him just how wrong the gift is.
jokes.setup: Explaining Johnny's terrible gift-giving history
jokes.punchline: The absurdly inappropriate gift for David combined with the systematic family approach to correction
jokes.explanation: Excellent character backstory that's both funny and revealing about family dynamics and David's obvious non-athletic nature.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: My dad once gave David a timeshare. For a prison.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: A timeshare for a prison?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And when we all told him how insane that was, he didn't apologize. He just gave David a book about prisons the next Christmas.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So his solution to a bad gift was... more prison content?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's called doubling down, Stevie. It's a Rose family tradition.

jokes.text: Like, David forced us to watch him shoot at a basket for 10 minutes, until the ball hit the rim, and then bounced back in his face.
jokes.setup: The basketball court story continuation
jokes.punchline: David's prolonged athletic failure ending in physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the basketball story with visual comedy that perfectly captures David's athletic ineptitude.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't understand why you're all looking at me like that. I'm perfectly capable of playing basketball.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, you literally tripped over the ball before the game even started.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That was a strategic move to throw off the other team.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Darling, the only thing you threw off was your dignity when you fell into the bleachers.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going back out there, and I'm going to show everyone what I'm made of.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, but maybe take off the cashmere first?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. This is who I—

jokes.text: But it actually worked out perfectly, because all David really wanted for his Bar Mitzvah, was a nose job.
jokes.setup: The basketball court failure story
jokes.punchline: Revealing David's actual Bar Mitzvah wish was plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that's perfectly in character - the casual revelation of a 13-year-old wanting cosmetic surgery is both shocking and fitting.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So David's Bar Mitzvah wish wasn't some spiritual thing or whatever?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I wanted a nose job.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, that's so sad. You were like thirteen and already thinking about your appearance?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Says the girl who got highlights in middle school.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's different. That's fashion. That's not... mutilating your face.

jokes.text: Oh, and also, he gave me a diamond tennis bracelet for my Sweet 16. - That's bad. - I know.
jokes.setup: Continuing the bad gift stories
jokes.punchline: A diamond bracelet being treated as a terrible gift shows their wealthy privilege
jokes.explanation: Perfect privilege comedy - what most people would consider an amazing gift is treated as obviously inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David gave me a diamond bracelet for my birthday.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That sounds nice.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It was white gold. I only wear yellow gold.

jokes.text: I've never been in this situation before. Where someone's been so nice... to me. And generous.
jokes.setup: David explaining his delay
jokes.punchline: The sad admission that kindness and generosity are foreign experiences
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny character revelation that explains David's behavior while highlighting his emotional growth.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was helping an elderly woman cross the street.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Which, I realize, sounds like a lie because kindness and generosity are foreign experiences for me.

jokes.text: So upset, that I barely finished the chocolates.
jokes.setup: David explaining how upset he was
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of being 'barely able' to finish luxurious gifts
jokes.explanation: Perfect David logic - his 'suffering' is measured by his reduced enjoyment of luxury items.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was so upset, I could barely finish my Cristal. I could barely finish my Hermès scarf. I could barely finish my weekend home in the Hamptons.

jokes.text: I just, I guess, didn't know, how many olive branches you were planning on extending. - Ideally, one!
jokes.setup: David's confusion about the gift-giving
jokes.punchline: Patrick's exasperated clarification that one apology should suffice
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy with Patrick's reasonable expectation contrasting with David's gift-trained mindset.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're saying I need to apologize twice?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: No, David. One apology. One gift is sufficient.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: But I already bought the second gift...

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Then return it.

jokes.text: I was lying about the chocolates, I ate them all.
jokes.setup: His earlier claim about barely finishing the chocolates
jokes.punchline: Admitting he actually devoured them completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals David's true gluttony while maintaining his dramatic flair.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I may have been slightly misleading about the chocolates. I didn't just finish them. I devoured them. Completely. I ate the entire box.

jokes.text: Um, while you're at lunch, can you get me some lunch?
jokes.setup: Patrick leaving to think about what David's done
jokes.punchline: David immediately making demands while supposedly being punished
jokes.explanation: Perfect David selfishness - unable to read the room and making practical demands during emotional moments.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, wait. Before you go, I need you to pick up some things from the store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And I'd prefer if you took the car that doesn't have the squeaky suspension.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, and could you grab my dry cleaning? The place closes at six.

jokes.text: You know what the great thing about this is? Right after work, I can do go a quick shift over at Bazonga's Gentleman's Lounge.
jokes.setup: Stevie wearing the excessive makeup Johnny gave her
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the makeup makes her suitable for strip club work
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Johnny how inappropriate his gift was through Stevie's deadpan extreme application.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I look like I'm ready to work the pole at a strip club.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you'd be very successful.

jokes.text: I'm getting flashbacks of David attempting a 3-pointer.
jokes.setup: The bad gift situation with Stevie
jokes.punchline: Reference to the basketball court disaster story
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the basketball story that shows Johnny is learning from his gift-giving mistakes.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, you know, Stevie, gift-giving is a delicate art. It requires thought, consideration, and an understanding of the recipient's needs and desires.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Which is why I'm confused about this basketball court.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That was one time.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It was a very generous gift, but perhaps not as well thought out as you might have hoped.

jokes.text: Sorry, so like, a singles event, then? - That word is now considered derogatory. I believe they prefer to be called independents.
jokes.setup: Clarifying the event concept
jokes.punchline: Moira's made-up political correctness about the word 'singles'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira pretension - inventing sensitivity around a perfectly normal word to sound sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so it's like a speed dating thing?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, we prefer to call it 'rapid-engagement social networking for the romantically unattached.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Why can't you just say singles?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Because 'single' is a divisive term that suggests a lack of wholeness. We're all whole people, Alexis.

jokes.text: So you stole my idea, took it to Council, and claimed it as your own? - Alexis, now is not the time for pettifogging!
jokes.setup: Alexis rightfully accusing Moira of theft
jokes.punchline: Moira dismissing valid criticism with an obscure vocabulary word
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira deflection using a fancy word ('pettifogging') to avoid accountability for obvious plagiarism.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You stole my sweater!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I didn't steal it, I merely engaged in some light kleptomania. It's not a crime, it's a condition.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That literally is a crime.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I find your accusation rather pellucid and frankly, beneath us.

jokes.text: Well then, I took your little germ of an idea, and I fertilized it to fruition.
jokes.setup: Moira defending taking credit for Alexis's idea
jokes.punchline: Grossly biological metaphor for idea development
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully disgusting imagery for creative collaboration. Very Moira to make it sound both pretentious and biological.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I carried the idea. I gestated it, I nurtured it, and I gave birth to it in front of a room full of people. That makes it my baby.

jokes.text: Starting to get keyed up about this prospect of a mother-daughter power team. Modern day Judy and Lorna.
jokes.setup: Moira getting excited about working with Alexis
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Judy and Lorna' - likely Judy Garland and Lorna Luft, an obscure showbiz reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Moira making an obscure showbiz reference that most people wouldn't get.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This is going to be just like Judy and Lorna!

jokes.text: I don't know who that is. - Oh my god.
jokes.setup: Moira's Judy and Lorna reference
jokes.punchline: Alexis's honest admission and Moira's horror at the cultural ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy with Alexis's cultural gap and Moira's theatrical disappointment.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Do you know who Judy Garland and Lorna Luft are?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Um, no. Should I?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis!

jokes.text: Consider this my olive branch.
jokes.setup: David's gesture after their fight about olive branches
jokes.punchline: Presumably giving Patrick an actual olive branch or related gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the olive branch discussion with David presumably making a literal or creative gesture.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to go find an actual olive branch and just hand it to you silently.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That's the most David thing I've ever heard.

jokes.text: You know people can see you, right?
jokes.setup: Someone (likely David) doing something embarrassing publicly
jokes.punchline: Alexis pointing out the obvious public nature of the display
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment with Alexis being the voice of reason about public embarrassment.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I can't believe I just did that in front of everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, you did it in front of literally everyone, so...

tmdb_overview: David tries to reconcile with Patrick. Moira pitches a singles event at Town Hall while Johnny buys Stevie a gift for her help at the motel.
insight: Family reconciliation fuels 45 jokes across tense negotiation scenes.
